entry.title: 【Course】K111E - Introduction to Management (E)
entry.content:
Introductory concepts

  • Management of what?

    • organization is a coordinated group of people who function to achieve particular goals.
    • it exist because particular goals cannot achieve by individuals.

  • Manager

    • a manager is a person who plans, organizes, leads, and controls (POLC) the allocation of organizational resources in pursuit of organization goals.
    • directs a group of people.
    • allocate other organizational resources.
    • differ from individual contributors.

POLC

  • Plan
  • Organizes
  • Leads
  • Controls

Six core managerial competencies

  • strategic competency
  • Planning competency
  • Multicultural competency
  • Teamwork competency
  • Communication competency
  • Self-management competency

History of Management thought

  • Traditional viewpoints of management

    1. Bureaucratic management
      Focuses on detailed rules and procedures to guide the behavior of employees.
      Benefits: efficiency and consistency / best for routine tasks and standardized service.
      cost: no freedom and creativity / slow decision making.

    2. Scientific management

      Focuses on objective observation and experimentation to increase efficiency.
      Benefits: efficiency and productivity / best for routine tasks and standardized service.
      cost: neglect of social needs.

    3. Administrative management

      Focuses on application of specific management principles to achieve efficiency.
      Benefits: professionalization of managerial roles.
      cost: overemphasis on rational behavior of managers.

  • Behavioral (Human relations) viewpoints of management

    Focuses on dealing effectively with the human aspects of organization to increase team performance.
    people do not just work for money.

  • System viewpoints of management

    This management viewpoints stresses the need to consider the potential effects of local decisions on the whole organizational system.

  • Contingency viewpoints of management

    This management viewpoints suggests that management practices should be consistent with the specific requirements of the situation: external environment?, product/service?. HR capability?

  • Quality viewpoints of management

    Total quality management(TQM)
    Focuses on making quality values the driving force behind leadership and any initiative.

Ethics

  • A set of values and principles the define right and wrong conduct.

  • Influences on ethical conduct.

    1. cultural influences
    2. legal influences
    3. organizational influences
    4. individual influences

  • Approaches to ethical issues.

    • Justice Approach
    • Moral right Approach
    • Utilitarian Approach

Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR)

  • benefits

    • Satisfied Stakeholders
    • Higher stock prices
    • Higher long-term performance

  • ESG factors

    • Environmental
    • Social
    • Governance

  • Stakeholders

    • Employees
    • Customers
    • Shareholders
    • Suppliers

  • Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)

Business environment

  • Economy
    • Low-cost manufacturing → Value-added features
    • Self-reliance → Offshoring, outsourcing
    • Domestic competition → Borderless competition
    • Physical, local store → Online, global store
    • Physical labor → knowledge labor
    • Smoke-stack industries → Environmental stewardship
    • Analog operations → Digital transformation
    • Company produces, customer only buys → Co-creations
  • Demographics
  • Culture Values
  • Politics
  • Technology
  • Competitive
    • Five Forces Model / Industry Analysis Model
      • Threat of new entrants
      • Threat of substitute Products of services
      • Bargaining Power of suppliers
      • Bargaining power of buyers
      • Rivalry among competitors

Formulating Plans and Strategies

  • Vision

    • Expresses fundamental aspirations, what the organization wants to become / do in the future.

  • Mission

    • Express the reason for existing, what the organization is supposed to be / do now.

  • Overall goals

    • Express the results that organizational members are committed to achieving for the long-term survival and growth of the organization.

  • Strategies

    • Express the courses of action required to achieve goals.

  • Resource allocation

    • Through Budgets.

  • SWOT Analysis

    • Strengths
    • Weaknesses
    • Opportunities
    • Threats

Strategics Alliances

  • the concept of Strategic Alliance
    • MA is not a SA.
  • management theories tha explain SA
    • Resource-based Theory
    • Transaction-cost Theory
  • general taxonomy of SA
  • Common forms of SA
    • Sales cooperation
    • Technology licensing
    • Joint development
    • Manufacturing consignment
    • Joint venture

Organizational Structure

  • Organizational Structure

    • division / differentiation
    • integration

  • Vertical design

    • Hierarchical levels
    • span of control
    • Authority / responsibility / accountability
    • centralization decentralization

  • Horizontal design

    • functional structure
    • product structure
    • geographical structure
    • matrix structure

  • Structural designs comparison

  • organizational activities

    • Producing activities
    • selling activities
    • supporting activities

Organizational Teams

  • Groups via teams

  • types of work teams

    • functional work teams
    • multidisciplinary work teams
    • problem solving teams
    • self-managing work teams

  • team effectiveness criteria

    • team performance
    • team processes
    • satisfaction of individual members
    • team preparedness for future

  • Interpersonal TRUST

    • affective-based trust
    • cognitive-based trust

  • stages of work team development

    • adjourning
    • performing
    • norming
    • storming
    • forming

Dynamics of Leadership

  • Regardless of position or rank, anyone can be a leader.

  • There is formal and informal leadership.

  • There are many levels of leadership

    • group leader
    • functional leader
    • business-unit leader
    • corporate leader
    • …

  • There have been an evolution in leadership theories

    • trait theories
    • behavioral theories
    • contingency theories

  • trait theories

    • Trait theories focus on personality, social or intellectual traits that differentiate leaders from non leaders.
    • exp.charismatic, enthusiastic, self-confident, intelligent…
    • Limitation: cause and effect relationships are not clear.

  • behavioral theories

    • behavioral theories focus on specific behaviors that differentiate leaders from non leaders.
    • limitation: changes in situations are not considered.

  • contingency theories

    • pic

  • Other views

    • a transformational leader inspires by providing a vision, giving personal attention, and stimulating their intellectual capacity.
    • a transformational leader constantly tries to align the followers personal goals with the organizations goals

motivating employees

  • the concept of motivation
    • willingness to make some effort toward a goal, conditioned by the goals potential to satisfy some individual need.
  • theories of motivation

    • Needs

      • In organizations, it is important to understand the basic need of employees and satisfy them, to varying degrees, with specific motivational practices.

    • X or Y

      • X: the assumption that employees dislike work, and not responsible, and must be pushed to perform.
      • Y: the assumption that employees like work, and responsible, and perform well on their own.

    • Goal-setting

      • What, How much, when
      • specific

    • Expectancy

      • good - reward
      • bad - penalty

    • MAO

      • Motivation
      • Ability
      • Opportunity

Achieving organizational control

  • To achieve strategic goals.

    • objective control
    • complete control
    • timely control
    • necessary control

  • Type of control

    • Output controls
    • Behavioral controls
    • Clan controls

  • source of control

    • stakeholders control
    • organizational controls
    • group controls
    • individual self-controls

entry.title: 【UnityTips】Variable-angle sector indicator / UnityTips之可变角度的扇形指示器
entry.content:
Create the Sector Indicator

  • Create a Plane / 创建一个Plane

  • Create a Material / 创建一个Material

  • Create a Shader with the following content. / 创建一个Shader，内容如下：

    Shader "Custom/Indicator" {    Properties {          _MainTex("Main Texture", 2D) = "white" {}        _Color ("Color", Color) = (0.17,0.36,0.81,0.0)        _Angle ("Angle", Range(0, 360)) = 60        _Gradient ("Gradient", Range(0, 1)) = 0    }    SubShader {    Tags { "Queue"="Transparent" "RenderType"="Transparent" "IgnoreProjector"="True" }        Pass {            ZWrite Off            Blend SrcAlpha OneMinusSrcAlpha            CGPROGRAM            #pragma vertex vert            #pragma fragment frag            #include "UnityCG.cginc"            sampler2D _MainTex;            float4 _Color;            float _Angle;            float _Gradient;            struct fragmentInput {                float4 pos : SV_POSITION;                float2 uv : TEXTCOORD0;            };            fragmentInput vert (appdata_base v)            {                fragmentInput o;                o.pos = mul (UNITY_MATRIX_MVP, v.vertex);                o.uv = v.texcoord.xy;                return o;            }            fixed4 frag(fragmentInput i) : SV_Target {                float distance = sqrt(pow(i.uv.x - 0.5, 2) + pow(i.uv.y - 0.5, 2));                if(distance > 0.5f){                    discard;                }                float grediant = (1 - distance - 0.5 * _Gradient) / 0.5;                fixed4 result = tex2D(_MainTex, i.uv) * _Color * fixed4(1,1,1, grediant);                float x = i.uv.x;                float y = i.uv.y;                float deg2rad = 0.017453;                  if(_Angle > 180){                    if(y > 0.5 && abs(0.5 - y) >= abs(0.5 - x) / tan((180 - _Angle / 2) * deg2rad))                        discard;                }                else                 {                    if(y > 0.5 || abs(0.5 -y) < abs(0.5 - x) / tan(_Angle / 2 * deg2rad))                        discard;                }                return result;            }            ENDCG        }    }      FallBack "Diffuse"}

  • Assign Shader to Material and Material to Plane. / 将Shader赋予Material，将Material赋予Plane。

Modify the Sector Indicator

  • 改变颜色
gameObject.GetComponent<Renderer>().material.color = Color.red;
